How solar powers your world

Solar Battery Storage Solutions – Power Your Home Day & Night | OMC Australia

Why Choose OMC Solar

Smarter Solar. Reliable Results.

At OMC Group, we make clean, renewable energy simple.
Our solar systems harness the power of the Australian sun and convert it into reliable electricity for your home or business — helping you save money while protecting the environment.

Contact Us
Learn More

But how does solar actually work

At OMC Group, we make clean, renewable energy simple.
Our solar systems harness the power of the Australian sun and convert it into reliable electricity for your home or business — helping you save money while protecting the environment.

Here’s a simple breakdown

Solar Panels Capture Sunlight

Solar panels, made up of photovoltaic (PV) cells, absorb sunlight throughout the day and convert it into direct current (DC) electricity — the first step in generating renewable power.
The brighter the sun, the more energy your system produces.

Inverter Converts DC to AC

The electricity generated by your panels is in DC form, which isn’t suitable for household use.
A solar inverter converts it into alternating current (AC) — the type of electricity that powers your lights, appliances, and devices.

Powering Your Home or Business

Once converted, the AC electricity flows through your switchboard to power your property.
Any excess energy can either:
⚡ Be exported back to the grid, earning feed-in credits, or
🔋 Be stored in a solar battery for later use.

Store Excess Energy with Solar Batteries

By adding a solar battery system, you can store surplus energy generated during the day and use it at night or during outages — giving you greater energy independence and long-term savings.

Monitor & Manage Your Energy

Our smart monitoring systems allow you to track energy production and usage in real time from your phone or computer — keeping you fully in control of your energy.

Why Solar Makes Sense in Australia

Lower electricity costs
Cleaner, sustainable energy
Increased property value
Government rebates and incentives

Still have questions? Reach out to our team.
